Greek-Entry.English-Entry: rings through which the reins pass
to be shut up, kept at home
attendant in a lady"s chamber, waiting-maid
the hole in the ship"s side, through which this oar worked
one of the rowers on the lowest bench
women"s apartment, inner part of the house
a special chamber in this part of the house
the lowest, darkest part of the ship, the hold
strike the sea with the oar, make a splash
to be master of the sea
made to wander o"er the sea, seatost
to be busy with the sea
one who works on the sea, a fisherman
to be full of heat, vigorous
that which may be ventured on
feelconfidence against, have no fear of
money paid to see conjurers" tricks
to be regarded as a marvel
fustic, Rhus Cotinus, used for dyeing yellow
place for seeing from, seat in the theatre
priest who carried images of the gods
to be made a show of, held up to shame,
like the spectators in a theatre
rule exercised by the spectators in a theatre
one who sells seats in a theatre
the acts of the gods, course of providence
that in which a valuable prize is proposed
to lay the foundation of, found firmly
allowed by the laws of God and men, righteous
the two muscles forming the borders of the palm
infatuation sent by the gods, madness
with the tongue of a god
by which the gods are injured
at the hands of the gods
a place above the stage where gods appeared
discourse on the gods and cosmology
oration in praise of a god
one who discourses of the gods
spell for evoking a divine revelation,
one who has a spirit of prophecy, an inspired person
destined by the gods, imparted by them
the persons who celebrated such a festival,
the art of making statues of gods
public messenger sent to inquire of an oracle
bless you! good heavens! for heaven"s sake!
wise in the things of God
festival at Delphi, at which the statues of Apollo and other gods were shown to the people
a revealer of God, a priest
dear to the gods, highly favoured
to be possessed by a god, inspired
service done to gain favour, paying court
one who serves the gods, worshipper
inclined to take care of, careful of
to be an attendant, do service
one who brought the hot water atbaths,
doing hot and hasty acts, reckless
that which is laid down, law, ordinance
αἱ Κῷαι, παρὰ Φιλητᾷ, καὶ αἱ φαρμακίδες
filled with the words of God, inspired
such as you would cut for a hungry Thessalian
to be sent to consult an oracle
being a spectator at the theatre
fund for providing free seats at public spectacles
envoy sent to consult an oracle
one who gazes at, an admirer
to be full of, abound in
to promote the generation of females, dog-mercury, Mercurialis perennis
hunting of wild beasts, the chase
instrument of the chase, net, trap
in the form of a beast
park where wild beasts are kept
come to the full size of a beast
full of wild beasts, infested by them
heralded by the roar of wild beasts
having the forms of wild beasts
clad in the skins of beasts
in the form of a beast
clad in the skins of beasts
lay up in store, preserve, pickle
one who lays up in store
accustomed to lay up in store
tax levied for the protection of granaries,
one who is always in the Theseum
Κατάλ. περιγραφικός, Γλυπτὰ τοῦ Ἐθνικοῦ Μους
to be in mortal fear of
maintaining the mortality of the soul
round building with conical roof, rotunda
the vessel in which this ink is retained
to be thrown into disorder, confused
the confused noise of a crowded assembly, uproar, clamour
with which one rushes to the fight
one who keeps going to Thrace
to be stretched on the tanner"s board, tanned
rower on the topmost of the three benches
bold in contriving, daring in design
to be bold, confident, take courage
carry out a coup de main
falling off of hair in patches
return made by children for their rearing
τὸ θρασύ, οὐκ ἔνι μοι τὸ θ
like a dirge, fit for a dirge
one who sings a dirge, mourner
τῆς ἁμάξης ἐφ" ᾧ τὰ ἀγόμενα ἐπιτίθεται
topmost course of stones in a wall, cornice, coping
one who throws pebbles into the divining-urn, soothsayer
mixture of eggs, milk, lard, flour, honey, cheese
λίπος: ἑορτὴ Ἀπόλλωνος, καὶ ἡ σύντροφος αὐτοῦ
herbs used as drugs and charms
make a confused noise, chatter, babble
noise as of many voices, murmur
that which is broken off, bit
to be coy and prudish, bridle up
to be carried by a storm
part of a victim offered in burntsacrifice
sack in which the eggs of the tunny are enveloped
κλάδους ἢ φύλλα, ἢ ἑορτὴ Ἀφροδίτης
cakes in the form of animals
as if one had eaten savory
burn so as to produce smoke
bold enough to slay a lion,
mixture of thyme with honey and vinegar
drink of thyme, vinegar, and brine
the thymus gland in the neck or breast
the principle of life, feeling and thought
mind, soul, as the seat of thought
spear a tunny-fish, strike with a harpoon
an offering of the first tunny-fish caught
with the head of a tunny-fish
look-out to watch for shoals of tunnies
from out of doors, from abroad
live in the open air, camp out
living out of doors, camping out
contest in which shields were borne
worn to commemorate the victory at Thyrea
to be armed with the oblong shield
for whom all doors were open
the opening in the os pubis
knock at the door, break it open
he who raves with the thyrsus
wand wreathed in ivy and vine-leaves with a pine-cone at the top
festival, at which sacrifices were offered
the tower on the back of elephants
arm oneself, put one"s harness on
